title: CRISPR-Cas systems and methods for altering expression of gene products
abstract: Methods and compositions for altering expression of gene products using engineered CRISPR-Cas systems in eukaryotic cells, including guide RNA and Cas9 nuclease components.

title: Methods and compositions for RNA-directed target DNA modification and for RNA-directed modulation of transcription
assignee: University of California / University of Vienna
abstract: A method of modifying a target DNA using a Cas9 polypeptide and a single-molecule guide RNA to introduce site-specific double-strand breaks for genome editing.

title: Methods of modifying a target nucleotide sequence with a CRISPR/Cas9 system
abstract: Compositions and methods for genome editing in mammalian cells using CRISPR/Cas9 with paired nickases to reduce off-target effects.

title: CRISPR-Cas component systems, methods and compositions for sequence manipulation
abstract: Engineered CRISPR-Cas systems delivered via viral vectors for in vivo genome editing of therapeutic target genes in mammalian tissues.

title: Base editing of nucleobases without double-stranded DNA cleavage
abstract: Fusion proteins combining a catalytically impaired Cas domain with a deaminase enable precise base editing without introducing double-strand breaks, for therapeutic correction of point mutations.

title: Prime editing compositions and methods for precise genome editing
abstract: A prime editor comprising a Cas nickase fused to a reverse transcriptase and a prime editing guide RNA installs targeted insertions, deletions, and substitutions without double-strand breaks.

title: In vivo gene editing of blood progenitors for treatment of hemoglobinopathies
abstract: Methods for ex vivo and in vivo editing of BCL11A enhancer in hematopoietic stem cells to induce fetal hemoglobin for the treatment of sickle cell disease and beta-thalassemia.

title: Lipid nanoparticle delivery of CRISPR-Cas for in vivo hepatic gene editing
abstract: Lipid nanoparticle formulations delivering Cas9 mRNA and guide RNA to the liver for in vivo knockout of the TTR gene to treat transthyretin amyloidosis.
